page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized server instance. It features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The underlying hardware configuration allocates 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

This instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, and the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The c8ib.2xlarge offers a compute-focused resource profile, prioritizing CPU speed and dedicated allocation over memory capacity or local storage. This design provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require consistent CPU execution without the overhead of unused memory or storage resources. Suitable workloads include compute-intensive tasks, batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics that rely on external storage systems.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated virtual CPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel processor running at 3.9 GHz, the instance utilizes 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The hardware configuration provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators included. Network performance is established at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Lacking local storage, this instance relies on network-attached storage solutions. The c8ib.2xlarge is designed for compute-bound workloads, such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics, where dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speeds are prioritized over large memory footprints or local disk space.
